courseleaf.title: Dramatic Art, Master of Fine Arts
courseleaf.informationtext:
Graduate Study

The Dramatic Art program has suspended admissions for the current admissions year.

The Department of Theatre & Dance offers programs of study and research leading to the M.F.A. in Dramatic Art (the interdisciplinary weaving of acting, directing, design, choreography and practice and research) and contributing to the Graduate Group Ph.D. in Performance Studies. Detailed information may be obtained by contacting the Graduate Program Administrators. For the M.F.A. in Theatre & Dance, 530-752-8710; Graduate Group in Performance Studies, 530-754-6973.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Dramatic Art.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Are you ready for a program of rigorous, advanced training, and practice that enables mid-career artists to explore and expand skills in order to develop artistically and professionally?  The Master of Fine Arts in Dramatic Art program offers artists the opportunity to create new dimensions of classical and contemporary performance.
